LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES INC. AND SCOTT MUNRO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO MAY 2, 2014 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 TO PERMIT PLAINTIFF RASHID ALATRAQCHI THE OPPORTUNITY TO FILE WITH SAN FRANCISCO SUPERIOR COURT HIS FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT THAT WAS FILED IN THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE NORTHERN DISTRICT OF CALIFORNIA ON SEPTEMBER 10, 2013 IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE CORRECTED ORDER ISSUED ON APRIL 1, 2014 BY JUDGE ROBERTSON. DEFENDANTS SHALL PROVIDE COURTESY COPY OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN APRIL 25, 2014 AT 4 PM PER LOCAL RULE 2.6C. PLAINTIFF RASHID ALATRAQCHI'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANTS' RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S DISCOVERY; REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS CONTINUED TO MARCH 10, 2014 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302. JUDGE PRO TEM SHALL PREPARE A REPORT AND RECOMMENDATION. JUDGE PRO TEM: PAUL A. RENNE; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED. (302)
MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF DEMURRER FILED BY DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES INC. MUNRO, SCOTT
ORDER DENYING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AFTER ARGUMENTS, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF RASHID ALATRAQCHI'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANTS' RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S DISCOVERY; REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. FIRST, PLAINTIFF FAILED TO FILE A SEPARATE STATEMENT ATTACHING THE DEFENDANTS' RESPONSES TO EITHER THE REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS OR THE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES TO WHICH HE IS SEEKING ANSWERS. (CAL. RULES OF COURT, RULE 3.1345(A).) WITHOUT SUCH A STATEMENT THE COURT HAS NO BASIS ON WHICH TO MAKE A RULING. SECOND, PLAINTIFF FAILED TO MAKE A GOOD FAITH ATTEMPT TO MEET AND CONFER FOLLOWING RECEIPT OF DEFENDANT COUNSEL'S MEET AND CONFER LETTER DATED JANUARY 27, 2014. (CODE CIV. PROC. 2016.040, 2030.300(B), 2031.310(B)(2). SEE O'CONNOR DECL., 12, 13, EXHIBIT F.) MONETARY SANCTIONS OF $ 1,500.00 ARE AWARDED IN FAVOR OF UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. AND AGAINST RASHID ALATRACQCHI TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF SERVICE OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-04-2014 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-05-2014 Continued To Aug-20-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-13-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: RASHID ALATRAQCHI, PLAINTIFF IN PROPRIA PERSONA.
ANDREW SPURCHISE, ESQ., 415.399.8438, FOR DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES INC. AND SCOTT MUNRO.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling re: DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES INC., SCOTT MUNRO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer to causes of action 1, 2, and 6 are sustained without leave to amend because plaintiff fails to allege exhaustion of administrative remedies. The demurrer to cause of action 5 is sustained without leave to amend because plaintiff does not clearly allege an employment relationship. The demurrer to cause of action 17 for work related stress is sustained without leave to amend because it is uncertain and plaintiff fails to demonstrate that it is a recognized cause of action. The demurrer to cause of action 7 for fraud is sustained without leave to amend because plaintiff does not allege the claim with the requisite particularity. The demurrer to cause of action 3 for intentional infliction of emotional distress is sustained without leave to amend because plaintiff does not clearly allege extreme and outrageous conduct. The demurrer to cause of action 4 for breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing is sustained without leave to amend because plaintiff does not clearly allege an underlying contract between the parties. The demurrer to causes of action 8, 10, and 11 for unfair competition is sustained without leave to amend because plaintiff does not clearly allege the unlawful, fraudulent, or unfair conduct. The demurrer to causes of action 9 and 12 is sustained without leave to amend because defendant is not a stranger to the parties' relationship. The demurrer to cause of action 13 for accounting is sustained without leave to amend because an accounting is a remedy. The demurrer to cause of action 14 for declaratory relief is sustained without leave to amend because plaintiff does not describe an actual controversy. The demurrer to causes of action 15 for injunction and 16 for writ of mandate is sustained without leave to amend because they are unintelligible and appear to relate to a non-party. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Joyce Finley #8620.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Defs' Responses To Pltf'S Discovery; Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-10-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Rashid Alatraqchi, Tel. 415.240.2262, plaintiff and moving party appearing on his own behalf;
Andrew M. Spurchise, Esq. of Little Mendelson PC, Tel. 415.399.8438, for defendant and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. After arguments, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF RASHID ALATRAQCHI'S Motion To Compel Defendants' Responses To Plaintiff's Discovery; Request For Monetary Sanctions is Denied. First, Plaintiff failed to file a separate statement attaching the defendants' responses to either the request for production of documents or the special interrogatories to which he is seeking answers. (Cal. Rules of Court, rule 3.1345(a).) Without such a statement the court has no basis on which to make a ruling. Second, Plaintiff failed to make a good faith attempt to meet and confer following receipt of Defendant counsel's meet and confer letter dated January 27, 2014. (Code Civ. Proc. 2016.040, 2030.300(b), 2031.310(b)(2). See O'Connor Decl., 12, 13, Exhibit F.)
Monetary sanctions of $ 1,500.00 are awarded in favor of Uber Technologies, Inc. and against Rashid Alatracqchi to be paid within 30 days of service of notice of entry of order. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.
